sentences.quote: Recent classical tensor-network methods --- belief propagation on 2D/3D PEPS networks (Tindall, Mello, Fishman, Stoudenmire, Sels, \textit{Science} \textbf{392}, 868 (2026)) and Chebyshev-TN density-matrix expansions on quasicrystal Hamiltonians (Antão, Sun, Fumega, Lado, \textit{PRL} \textbf{136}, 156601 (2026)) --- have collectively narrowed the gap between classical and quantum many-body simulation.
sentences.quote_normalized: recent classical tensor-network methods --- belief propagation on 2d/3d peps networks (tindall, mello, fishman, stoudenmire, sels, science 392, 868 (2026)) and chebyshev-tn density-matrix expansions on quasicrystal hamiltonians (antão, sun, fumega, lado, prl 136, 156601 (2026)) --- have collectively narrowed the gap between classical and quantum many-body simulation.
sentences.agent_notes: Both inline-cited Tindall-Sels Science 392/868 (2026) and Antão et al. PRL 136/156601 (2026) resolve in CITATION_REGISTRY. Both sources cached at Lit-Search/Phase-6w/primary-sources/. Year 2026 + journal/volume/page consistent with bibitem block.

meta.quote_normalized: recent classical tensor-network methods --- belief propagation on 2d/3d peps networks (tindall, mello, fishman, stoudenmire, sels, science 392, 868 (2026)) and chebyshev-tn density-matrix expansions on quasicrystal hamiltonians (antão, sun, fumega, lado, prl 136, 156601 (2026)) --- have collectively narrowed the gap between classical and quantum many-body simulation.

sentences.quote: We formalize their joint substrate in the Lean~4 proof assistant and ship a kernel-verified \textit{demarcation theorem}: a quantum many-body system on a factor graph $G$ with factor weights $f_a$ and categorical-Chern data $(c_0, c_1)$ is classically simulable in the combined BP-LDP / Chebyshev-TN regime if and only if (i) the large-deviation rate function of the loop-correction terms is below a computable threshold (the system's underlying topology is tree-like and factor weights are non-negative) AND (ii) the topological coefficient $c_1$ vanishes.
sentences.quote_normalized: we formalize their joint substrate in the lean~4 proof assistant and ship a kernel-verified demarcation theorem: a quantum many-body system on a factor graph $g$ with factor weights $f_a$ and categorical-chern data $(c_0, c_1)$ is classically simulable in the combined bp-ldp / chebyshev-tn regime if and only if (i) the large-deviation rate function of the loop-correction terms is below a computable threshold (the system's underlying topology is tree-like and factor weights are non-negative) and (ii) the topological coefficient $c_1$ vanishes.
sentences.agent_notes: The biconditional asserted matches the Lean module AnalogHawkingDemarcation.lean L101 'analog_hawking_quantum_advantage_demarcation'. The decomposition (tree-like ∧ non-neg-factor-weights) ↔ IsBPConvergenceFavorable verified at BPLDPSimulability.lean L195 'isBPConvergenceFavorable_on_tree_iff_factor_weights_nonneg'. c1=0 axis verified at ChernBridge.lean L? 'crystalline_eq_quasicrystalline_iff_c1_zero'.

meta.quote_normalized: we formalize their joint substrate in the lean~4 proof assistant and ship a kernel-verified demarcation theorem: a quantum many-body system on a factor graph $g$ with factor weights $f_a$ and categorical-chern data $(c_0, c_1)$ is classically simulable in the combined bp-ldp / chebyshev-tn regime if and only if (i) the large-deviation rate function of the loop-correction terms is below a computable threshold (the system's underlying topology is tree-like and factor weights are non-negative) and (ii) the topological coefficient $c_1$ vanishes.

sentences.quote: Tindall, Mello, Fishman, Stoudenmire, and Sels demonstrated that classical tensor-network contraction via belief propagation matches the D-Wave Advantage2 quantum annealer on 300+ qubit spin-glass dynamics across 2D square, 3D cubic, and 3D diamond lattices~\cite{TindallMelloFishmanStoudenmireSels2026Science392}.

sentences.quote: We make four main contributions:
sentences.quote_normalized: we make four main contributions:
sentences.agent_notes: Itemize setup.

meta.quote_normalized: we make four main contributions:

sentences.quote: A Lean~4 formalization of belief propagation on bipartite factor graphs in the Yedidia-Freeman-Weiss 2003 convention, with 24 substantive theorems covering message-update positivity, factorization, fixed-point stability, and Bethe free-energy substrate (Section~\ref{sec:bp-substrate}).
sentences.quote_normalized: a lean~4 formalization of belief propagation on bipartite factor graphs in the yedidia-freeman-weiss 2003 convention, with 24 substantive theorems covering message-update positivity, factorization, fixed-point stability, and bethe free-energy substrate (section~).
sentences.agent_notes: The numeric claim '24 substantive theorems in BeliefPropagation.lean' MATCHES grep '^theorem ' (24). PASS on count. HOWEVER citation 'Yedidia-Freeman-Weiss 2003 convention' references a bibitem 'YedidiaFreemanWeiss2003' which is NOT in CITATION_REGISTRY — Class IA + Class TN: citation drift. FAIL aggregates to citation-registry gap (see Discussion-level finding).

meta.quote_normalized: a lean~4 formalization of belief propagation on bipartite factor graphs in the yedidia-freeman-weiss 2003 convention, with 24 substantive theorems covering message-update positivity, factorization, fixed-point stability, and bethe free-energy substrate (section~).

sentences.quote: A kernel-verified biconditional characterization of LDP-controlled BP convergence: $\mathrm{IsBPConvergenceFavorable}\ G\ f_a \iff \mathrm{loopCorrectionRate}\ G \leq 1/2 \land (\forall a\, y,\, 0 \leq f_a(y))$ (Section~\ref{sec:ldp-bp}).
sentences.quote_normalized: a kernel-verified biconditional characterization of ldp-controlled bp convergence: $isbpconvergencefavorable\ g\ f_a \iff loopcorrectionrate\ g \leq 1/2 \land (\forall a\, y,\, 0 \leq f_a(y))$ (section~).
sentences.agent_notes: Headline biconditional verified at BPLDPSimulability.lean L183. The conjunction inside the biconditional decomposes via loopCorrectionRate_below_threshold_iff_tree (≤ 1/2 ↔ tree) and IsBPConvergenceFavorable definition (which bundles tree + non-neg-factor-weights). The displayed math statement matches the Lean statement.

meta.quote_normalized: a kernel-verified biconditional characterization of ldp-controlled bp convergence: $isbpconvergencefavorable\ g\ f_a \iff loopcorrectionrate\ g \leq 1/2 \land (\forall a\, y,\, 0 \leq f_a(y))$ (section~).

sentences.quote: A categorical-Chern $\leftrightarrow$ real-space-Chern bridge formalized via the Chebyshev tensor-network expansion, with substantively distinct crystalline (band-edge $x = 1$) and quasicrystalline (band-edge $x = -1$) limits whose difference $2 c_1$ extracts the topological invariant (Section~\ref{sec:chern-bridge}).
sentences.quote_normalized: a categorical-chern $\leftrightarrow$ real-space-chern bridge formalized via the chebyshev tensor-network expansion, with substantively distinct crystalline (band-edge $x = 1$) and quasicrystalline (band-edge $x = -1$) limits whose difference $2 c_1$ extracts the topological invariant (section~).
sentences.agent_notes: The crystalline/quasicrystalline limit story matches ChernBridge.lean docstring L13-L21 exactly: crystalline → c_0+c_1 (band-edge x=1), quasicrystalline → c_0-c_1 (band-edge x=-1), difference = 2c_1. All three named theorems exist (Substantive Theorem 3 explicitly named crystalline_minus_quasicrystalline_eq_twice_c1).

meta.quote_normalized: a categorical-chern $\leftrightarrow$ real-space-chern bridge formalized via the chebyshev tensor-network expansion, with substantively distinct crystalline (band-edge $x = 1$) and quasicrystalline (band-edge $x = -1$) limits whose difference $2 c_1$ extracts the topological invariant (section~).

sentences.quote: A unified demarcation theorem (Section~\ref{sec:demarcation}) combining the LDP-BP and Chern contributions: a system is classically simulable iff BP-LDP-favorable AND $c_1 = 0$.
sentences.quote_normalized: a unified demarcation theorem (section~) combining the ldp-bp and chern contributions: a system is classically simulable iff bp-ldp-favorable and $c_1 = 0$.
sentences.agent_notes: Verified at AnalogHawkingDemarcation.lean L120 'analog_hawking_simulable_iff_tree_and_nonneg_and_c1_zero'.

meta.quote_normalized: a unified demarcation theorem (section~) combining the ldp-bp and chern contributions: a system is classically simulable iff bp-ldp-favorable and $c_1 = 0$.

sentences.quote: The Phase 6w D7 bundle's central contribution is a kernel-verified demarcation between regimes where classical tensor-network methods suffice and where quantum processors retain a structural advantage.

sentences.agent_notes: Self-summary; consistent with verified headline theorem. NOTE: 'Phase 6w D7 bundle' is a project-internal phase reference; per Stage-13 hedging discipline external readers may need a one-sentence gloss in the published version, but flagged as recommend-not-blocker.

sentences.quote: The demarcation is falsifiable on two distinct axes and the substrate is generic enough to apply beyond analog Hawking to any LDP-controlled-BP / Chebyshev-TN classification setting.

blocking_issues.summary: Abstract '67 substantive theorems' contradicts grep total of 68 across the 7 Phase 6w modules — off by 1. Either the count is wrong or a substantive-vs-companion taxonomy needs to be made explicit (consistent reading is 7 substantive + 1 companion in BPLDPSimulability, giving 67 substantive + 1 companion = 68 total; not yet codified in prose).

3 of 5 paper bibitems missing from CITATION_REGISTRY: YedidiaFreemanWeiss2003, Zurek1985, BiancoResta2011. These are foundational refs (BP convention, Kibble-Zurek mechanism origin, Chern marker real-space construction). Must be added before Stage-13 close.

non_blocking_followups.summary: lean_deps.json + docs/counts.json are stale (last regen 2026-05-26 07:35 AM, pre-Phase-6w); Class TN lookups on Phase 6w theorem names false-negative against the cached registries. Authoritative check is direct grep on lean/SKEFTHawking/*.lean. Counts.json regen deferred per user direction; cache will refresh on next sweep.

Skeleton §2-§6 placeholders are intentional pending Stage-10 revision (per task brief). Each placeholder's inline identifiers verify against the underlying Lean source. UNGROUNDED verdicts on those sentences are coverage gaps, not chain failures.

Kernel-axiom-closure claim '[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]' on the 67 theorems is plausible (project-wide axiom count is 0 per counts.json) but the agent did not execute per-theorem #print axioms verification. Recommend a one-shot Bash verification pass against the 7 Phase 6w module entry points before submission.

Sections §2-§6 are skeleton placeholders; per task brief these will be expanded in subsequent Stage-10 revision. Recommend Stage-10 also explicitly enumerates which theorems are 'substantive' vs 'companion' in each module so the abstract arithmetic is reproducible.
